name: Kymera Therapeutics, Inc.
business: Kymera Therapeutics is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing oral small molecule medicines using targeted protein degradation, primarily for immune-inflammatory diseases.

exposures.sentence: We are a leader in targeted protein degradation (TPD), a next-generation small molecule therapeutic modality that engages the body's natural cellular recycling system to selectively eliminate disease-causing proteins.

catalysts.event: KT-621 Phase 2b data in atopic dermatitis
catalysts.sentence: Two parallel Phase 2b trials to evaluate KT-621 in patients with moderate to severe AD and asthma are ongoing with data expected by mid-2027 and late-2027, respectively.
Two parallel Phase 2b trials to evaluate KT-621 in patients with moderate to severe AD and asthma are ongoing with data expected by mid-2027 and late-2027, respectively.

catalysts.event: Sanofi to advance KT-485 into clinical testing
catalysts.sentence: Sanofi plans to advance KT-485/SAR447971 into clinical testing in 2026.

tamClaims.claim: More than 160 million patients in the US, Europe, and Japan are diagnosed with prevalent immune-inflammatory diseases that our programs may address.
tamClaims.sentence: We believe there are more than 160 million patients in the United States, Europe and Japan that are diagnosed with some of the most prevalent immune-inflammatory diseases that our programs have the potential to address, nearly half of whom remain untreated.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 3% of diagnosed moderate to severe inflammatory disease patients receive systemic advanced therapies.
tamClaims.sentence: As a result, only a small percentage of patients, which we believe to be approximately 3% of the diagnosed population with moderate to severe inflammatory diseases, are currently treated with systemic advanced therapies, mostly injectable biologics.

tamClaims.claim: More than 140 million people in the US, Europe, and Japan suffer from Type 2 inflammation-driven diseases, with about 48 million moderate to severe.
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate that more than 140 million people in the US, Europe and Japan suffer from diseases predominately driven by Type 2 inflammation, about 48 million of which can be characterized as moderate to severe.

tamClaims.claim: More than 10 million people in the US, Europe, and Japan are affected by IRF5-mediated diseases.
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate that more than 10 million people across the United States, Europe, and Japan are affected by diseases in which IRF5-mediated pathways are implicated in pathogenesis.
